intro: Stisseria dejecta, also known as Devil's Claw or Graskop, is a perennial vine in the Apocynaceae family native to southern Africa. This plant has been traditionally used to alleviate various health conditions, including pain, inflammation, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties. The bioactive constituents of Stisseria dejecta include harpagoside, a unique iridoid glycoside responsible for its medicinal properties.
